lines.text: (reading) [deliberate] We arrive as guests of history, to relieve it of its oldest burden...
[pause] The flag's gone. There's just the socket hole where it stood.
[quietly] There's fresh prints in here, laid right over the crew's.
(into radio) Harmony, Site One. The artifact is... [pause] not present.
[excited] There were NEVER any flags! Fifty years of soundstage and they're up there right now hunting for PROPS! Look at them! WAKE UP!
[tired] You can shut the cameras off. There's nothing to film here either.
Took 'em where, Buck? It's the Moon.
...and in just a few minutes now, these young people are going to watch us bring the very first flag back home.
[pause] They tell me we'll have the picture back for you very shortly.
Commander. [hesitates] The possibility must be raised. If the artifacts were never — if the landings themselves were—
There's wheel tracks running straight off that way, clear out to the horizon.
(then) Whoever they are, they're not hiding their driving.
